blockingIssues.description: contactStore is not wired into createProductionServerOptions() in src/relay/index.ts. The contact-policy routes (/contacts/status, /contacts/approve, /contacts/pending) and message send/inbox autonomy annotations are guarded by `&& contactStore`, so they are inactive in production relay startup. This means VAL-RELAY-012 and VAL-RELAY-013 behavior (first-contact autonomy gating and pending state for unknown senders) is not operational in real deployments, only in test-injected scenarios.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: When a feature adds a new dependency to RelayServerOptions in src/relay/server.ts, the entrypoint-wiring test (test/relay/entrypoint-wiring.test.ts) must be extended to assert that createProductionServerOptions() includes the new dependency. This prevents silent production integration regressions where test-only injection masks missing production wiring.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: autonomy-first-contact-pending-policy added contactStore to RelayServerOptions and guarded routes with `&& contactStore`, but did not wire it in src/relay/index.ts or extend entrypoint-wiring tests. The existing entrypoint-wiring tests only assert messageStore/tokenVerifier/participantStore/accountStore, so the gap was not caught.

rejectedObservations.observation: Skill procedure requires manual CLI/API/SSE sanity checks for user-facing changes, but handoff marks followedProcedure=true without interactiveChecks or skip rationale.
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a process compliance note for the worker, not a shared-state update. The skill procedure is already documented; enforcement is an orchestrator concern, not a library/services/AGENTS.md change.
